chunk_summary: Defines follow-up cadence and monitoring metrics for feeding support in neurodisability, stratified by risk tiers, with practical guidelines for Indian practice and tele-follow-up options.
learning_objectives: Understand the classification of risk tiers for feeding support in neurodisability.
Learn the recommended follow-up cadence for each risk tier, including numeric guidelines.
Identify standardized monitoring metrics for tracking feeding safety and efficiency.
Explore tele-follow-up strategies for caregivers facing travel barriers.
key_facts: Risk tiers are categorized as low, moderate, and high based on feeding safety, growth, and respiratory events.
High-risk patients require reviews every 2–4 weeks until stable, then every 2–3 months.
Moderate-risk patients need reviews every 6–8 weeks until stable, then every 3–6 months.
Low-risk patients should be reviewed every 6 months, with earlier reviews after respiratory infections.
Standardized metrics include EDACS, IDDSI levels, meal duration, hydration markers, and respiratory burden.
Tele-follow-up can be used for stability checks, with caregivers sharing videos for assessment.
reference_quote: "Define a risk tier at each visit using swallow safety, efficiency, respiratory events, and caregiver feasibility, then match review frequency to risk." (Source: ESPGHAN Advice Guide)
